description:
You are acting as a deployment engineer who is putting the finishing touches on the next production release that lives in the working copy located at  

/home/user/project  

The repository already contains a small Bash-based markdown linter:

• Executable script: /home/user/project/scripts/md_lint  
  – It prints its findings to STDOUT, one issue per line.

Your job: capture the linter’s output in a versionable log file so that the release manager can review it later.

Required actions (all must be achieved with **one single terminal command**):

1. Make sure the directory /home/user/project/build exists.  
2. Run the linter script (/home/user/project/scripts/md_lint).  
3. Redirect its output, together with a fixed header, into the file  
   /home/user/project/build/markdown_lint_report.txt

Required file format (exactly, line-for-line, using Unix LF endings):

Line 1   === Markdown Lint Report ===  
Line 2   (blank line – simply a lone LF character)  
Line 3+  The verbatim, unmodified STDOUT produced by /home/user/project/scripts/md_lint, in the order emitted by the script.  
No extra spaces at line ends and no additional blank line after the last linter message.

If executed correctly, the final file /home/user/project/build/markdown_lint_report.txt will be ready for commit and the automated checker will validate its bytes exactly. Do everything with one compound shell command; do not run intermediate commands or manually edit the file afterwards.
truth:
Initial repository state (already present before the agent starts):

1. /home/user/project/scripts/md_lint  
   • Permissions: 755  
   • Contents (with trailing LF on each line):  
     #!/usr/bin/env bash  
     echo "README.md:1 MD001/heading-increment Header levels should only increment by one level at a time"  
     echo "CHANGELOG.md:5 MD012/no-multiple-blanks Multiple consecutive blank lines"  

2. /home/user/project/README.md – any content, not relevant for validation.  
3. /home/user/project/CHANGELOG.md – any content, not relevant for validation.

What the checker will verify after the agent finishes:

• File /home/user/project/build/markdown_lint_report.txt exists.  
• File contents must be **exactly** (including the final newline, no extra blank line afterwards):

=== Markdown Lint Report ===
 
README.md:1 MD001/heading-increment Header levels should only increment by one level at a time
CHANGELOG.md:5 MD012/no-multiple-blanks Multiple consecutive blank lines

• Every line ends with a single LF character (Unix style).  
• No other files or directories are required; permissions can be default user-writable.
